{"credits": [{"type": "analyst", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1674512?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/weiznich/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/weiznich/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/weiznich/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/weiznich/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/weiznich", "id": 1674512, "login": "weiznich", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjE2NzQ1MTI=", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/weiznich/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/weiznich/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/weiznich/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/weiznich/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/weiznich/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/weiznich", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:H/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-20", "name": "Improper Input Validation"}], "description": "Diesel uses the `sqlite3_value_text` function to receive strings from SQLite while deserializing query results. We misinterpreted the corresponding [SQLite](https://sqlite.org/c3ref/value_blob.html) documentation that this function always returns a UTF-8 encoded string values as `*const c_char`. Based on that we used `str::from_utf8_unchecked` to construct a Rust string slice without any additional UTF-8 checks in place. It turned out that this function doesn't always return correct UTF-8 strings. For field of the SQLite side storage type `BLOB` this pointer can contain arbitrary bytes, which makes the usage of `str::from_utf8_unchecked` unsound as this violates the safety contract of `str` to only contain valid UTF-8 encoded Strings.

## Mitigation

The preferred mitigation to the outlined problem is to update to a Diesel version 2.3.8 or newer, which includes fixes for the problem.

## Resolution

Diesel now correctly checks whether the provides byte buffer is actually valid UTF-8, instead of relying on SQLite's documentation. This fix is included in the `2.3.8` release.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-h5x4-m2qf-r4f2", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-05T18:08:05Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h5x4-m2qf-r4f2", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-h5x4-m2qf-r4f2"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-05-05T18:08:05Z", "references": ["https://github.com/diesel-rs/diesel/pull/5042", "https://rustsec.org/advisories/RUSTSEC-2026-0111.html", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h5x4-m2qf-r4f2"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/diesel-rs/diesel", "summary": "Diesel's SQLite backend has possible UTF-8 corruption", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-18T20:21:41Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-h5x4-m2qf-r4f2", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2.3.8", "package": {"ecosystem": "rust", "name": "diesel"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2.3.8"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Diesel's SQLite backend has possible UTF-8 corruption
